statement: Based on Iceland's GDP and population, and using the methodology applied to current EU member states, Iceland's estimated gross contribution to the EU budget would be approximately €200–250 million per year. After expected receipts from CAP, structural funds, and other programmes, the net contribution is estimated at €100–180 million per year, making Iceland a net contributor.
source_name: Institute of International Affairs, University of Iceland — EU membership cost analysis
caveats: These are estimates based on extrapolation from existing member state contributions. Actual figures would depend on accession negotiations, any transitional arrangements, and the EU's multiannual financial framework in effect at the time of accession. Iceland's high GDP per capita means it would almost certainly be a net contributor.

related_entries.statement: Iceland currently contributes to the EEA/EFTA Grants mechanism, which provides approximately €180 million per year (shared among Iceland, Norway, and Liechtenstein) to reduce social and economic disparities in the EEA. Norway pays the vast majority. As an EU member, Iceland's estimated net budget contribution would be significantly higher, though the exact amount depends on GDP and negotiated rebates.

Under the 2021–2028 EEA/EFTA Financial Mechanism, Iceland's share of the EEA Grants is approximately €13 million per year (out of a total €1.55 billion package for the period). Norway contributes roughly 95% of the total. Iceland also makes separate bilateral contributions to specific beneficiary states.

Iceland's total EEA-related financial obligations (EEA Grants + EFTA budget + regulatory compliance costs) are estimated at roughly €20–30 million per year. This compares to an estimated net EU budget contribution of €100–180 million per year. However, EU membership would provide access to EU structural funds, CAP payments, research funding (Horizon Europe — currently accessed via association agreement), and Erasmus+ on full member terms.

citations.report_title: 25. þáttur Silfursins: Stefnt að kosningu um ESB í sumar

claims.claim_text: Kostnaður ESB-aðildarvegferðarinnar mun hlaupa á mörgum milljörðum króna.

citations.report_title: Snorri segir húsnæði verða jafndýrt þótt vextir lækki

claims.claim_text: Ísland sé mjög eftirsóknarvert fyrir Evrópusambandið að fá inn vegna sterks efnahagslífs.
